original_text: R&D investment remains a strategic priority for Amazon, with FY2025 R&D expenses reaching $108,521,000,000 [CHUNK_ID: 534dacac-29c9-481c-8845-b786f27f197d], up from $88,544,000,000 in FY2024 [CHUNK_ID: a34693a5-e557-42e6-92f5-aa5998f8da7f], $85,622,000,000 in FY2023 [CHUNK_ID: 2fdd530a-a7d5-4c8e-aaa5-10ab743173e5], and $73,213,000,000 in FY2022 [CHUNK_ID: 0eece3ef-3e1b-4c42-935a-1d34e429cf70]. FY2022 R&D intensity was 14.24% of revenue [CALC_ID: 067e0511-a49a-4cdb-847f-c0470722fc7b], while FY2025 R&D intensity appears to have increased to approximately 15.1% based on the revenue and R&D figures provided. This elevated R&D spending intensity indicates continued investment in AWS infrastructure, AI capabilities, and logistics automation to sustain competitive advantages across Amazon's diversified business portfolio.
revised_text: R&D investment remains a strategic priority for Amazon, with projected FY2025 R&D expenses of $108,521,000,000 [CHUNK_ID: 534dacac-29c9-481c-8845-b786f27f197d], up from $88,544,000,000 in FY2024 [CHUNK_ID: a34693a5-e557-42e6-92f5-aa5998f8da7f], $85,622,000,000 in FY2023 [CHUNK_ID: 2fdd530a-a7d5-4c8e-aaa5-10ab743173e5], and $73,213,000,000 in FY2022 [CHUNK_ID: 0eece3ef-3e1b-4c42-935a-1d34e429cf70]. FY2022 R&D intensity was 14.24% of revenue [CALC_ID: 067e0511-a49a-4cdb-847f-c0470722fc7b]. This elevated R&D spending intensity indicates continued investment in AWS infrastructure, AI capabilities, and logistics automation to sustain competitive advantages across Amazon's diversified business portfolio.
changes_summary: 1) Added 'projected' qualifier to FY2025 R&D expenses to clarify forward-looking nature given July 2025 as-of date (addresses Issue 1 - scope_violation: FY2025 full-year data would not be available until early 2026). 2) Removed unsupported FY2025 R&D intensity claim of 15.1% as no calculation ID exists to verify this figure (addresses Issue 2 - unsupported_claim). 3) Retained all historical FY2022-2024 R&D expense data with proper chunk citations. 4) Retained FY2022 R&D intensity (14.24%) with its calculation ID citation. 5) Kept strategic analysis commentary as it represents analytical interpretation rather than factual claims requiring citation.
